message:
Last winter, I casually mentioned to my partner that we should grab some extra blankets and batteries 'just in case' of a bad storm. He smiled politely and moved on. Three weeks later, a surprise ice storm knocked out power for 18 hours—and I got to be *that person* who said "I told you so" while we were huddled under those exact blankets by candlelight.

Honestly? I felt terrible about it. Not because I was wrong, but because I realized prep doesn't have to be some big dramatic thing—it's just... thinking ahead a little. A few basics tucked away mean nobody panics, nobody scrambles, and nobody has to rehash the "I told you so" conversation ever again.

Do you have a moment like that—where a little prep actually saved the day?
